mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns exceed 60% of the time when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment rate increase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in consumer spending and residential construction confirms broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio or Rank IC below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side recovery, generating positive short-term market momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 65% of periods where both inflation and rates exceed the specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market tightness and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend confirms robust aggregate demand, prompting immediate equity market repricing and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in 60% or more of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial output and total employment signal a broad economic slowdown, triggering defensive portfolio shifts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.
